description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 7 AI-platform-ops cluster: model-behavior eval harness vs code-test curation vs threat modeling vs live monitoring, and against the shipped regression-suite-curator, qa-automation-architect, api-contract-test-designer, and the ai-security-red-team-reviewer subagent.

cases.prompt: Create a golden dataset and graders to measure our chatbot's quality, safety, and hallucination rate, and block regressions on model upgrades.
cases.reason: Evaluating model behavior across quality/safety dimensions with a dataset and graders is this skill; the QA skills curate and structure CODE tests, not model-output evals.

cases.prompt: Decide which of our existing automated tests belong in the smoke tier and which to retire, against our runtime budget.
cases.reason: Promote/retain/retire decisions over the code regression suite are regression-suite-curator; this skill curates the model-eval dataset, a different artifact.

cases.prompt: Figure out what attacks our AI feature is exposed to across the OWASP LLM Top 10 before we test anything.
cases.reason: Enumerating the threats is ai-threat-modeler; this skill encodes and runs the resulting red-team cases, it doesn't produce the threat model.

cases.prompt: Instrument our production AI endpoint with alerts on error rate, latency, and cost spikes.
cases.reason: Live production instrumentation and alerting is observability-operator; this skill evaluates behavior pre-merge against a dataset, not live traffic.

cases.prompt: Verify our provider's API responses still match the schema our consumers depend on and gate breaking changes.
cases.reason: API request/response contract verification is api-contract-test-designer; this skill validates model output quality/safety, not API-shape compatibility between services.
